item.title: October 12th, 1911 – Harvey Ervin Witwer Buys Idlewild Lot 4 and Part Lot 2
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post October 12th, 1911 – Harvey Ervin Witwer Buys Idlewild Lot 4 and Part Lot 2 appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
Harvey Witwer and wife, Bertha Stow, buy Idlewild Lot 4 and part of Lot 2 from Maryette Bartholf, mother of Ella Van Bramer.

Bartholf, Maryette – Witwer, Harvey E & Bertha S, $1; L 4 & pt L 2 Idle Wild sub.

The Fort Collins Express – October 12, 1911

The post October 12th, 1911 – Harvey Ervin Witwer Buys Idlewild Lot 4 and Part Lot 2 appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.

item.title: August 3rd, 1911 – Ella Van Bramer Transfers Titles of Idlewild Lots 1, 2 and 4
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post August 3rd, 1911 – Ella Van Bramer Transfers Titles of Idlewild Lots 1, 2 and 4 appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
Ella Van Bramer Transfers Titles of Idlewild Lots 1, 2 and 4 to her mother, Maryette Bartholf.

VanBramer, Ella G – Bartholf, Maryette, $1; L 1 2 4 Idle Wild sub.

The Fort Collins Express – August 3, 1911

The post August 3rd, 1911 – Ella Van Bramer Transfers Titles of Idlewild Lots 1, 2 and 4 appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.

item.title: July 13th, 1911 – Ella Van Bramer Sells Idlewild Lot 5
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post July 13th, 1911 – Ella Van Bramer Sells Idlewild Lot 5 appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
Ella Van Bramer sells Idlewild Lot 5 and the “Idlewild House” to C. S. Ickes.

Van Bramer, Ella G – Ickes, C S, $50; L 5 Idle Wild sub.

The Fort Collins Express – July 13, 1911

The post July 13th, 1911 – Ella Van Bramer Sells Idlewild Lot 5 appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.

item.title: May 18th, 1911 – Ella Van Bramer Sells Idlewild Lot 3
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post May 18th, 1911 – Ella Van Bramer Sells Idlewild Lot 3 appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
Ella Van Bramer sells Idlewild Lot 3 and the Idlewild Lodge to A M Swain and J A Hill.

Van Bramer, Ella G-Swain, A M & Hill, J A, $500; L3 Idle Wild sub-

The Fort Collins Express – May 18, 1911

The post May 18th, 1911 – Ella Van Bramer Sells Idlewild Lot 3 appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.

item.title: May 13th, 1892 – John Rannells Organizes a Company to Build a Toll Road through the Big Thompson
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post May 13th, 1892 – John Rannells Organizes a Company to Build a Toll Road through the Big Thompson appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
John Rannells organizes a corporation, “The People’s Toll-Road Mining and Manufacturing Company”, to build a Toll Road through the Big Thompson Canyon, which would pass through present day Idlewild.

To Middle Park – A Toll Road to Be Built There from Loveland

A corporation, with a strong financial backing, has been organized to build a toll road from Loveland to Middle Park, following the Big Thompson River to the park, and thence over the range. This is the finest scenic, as well as the most practical route from the plains to the gem of the mountains. the corporation is composed of parties form Larimer and Boulder counties. We are given to understand that the road will be completed before the tourist season of ’92 sets in. The Leader will keep its readers thoroughly posted on this matter.

The Leader – May 13th, 1892

The post May 13th, 1892 – John Rannells Organizes a Company to Build a Toll Road through the Big Thompson appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.

item.title: June 21st, 1906 – Ella Van Bramer Advertises Idlewild
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post June 21st, 1906 – Ella Van Bramer Advertises Idlewild appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
The first advertisement for Ella Van Bramer’s “Iydlewilde,” a summer resort with fishing, tents, and home cooked meals.

Advertisement for Ella Van Bramer’s “Iydlewilde” Summer Resort. From the Loveland Register, June 21st, 1906.

The post June 21st, 1906 – Ella Van Bramer Advertises Idlewild appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.

item.title: December 15th, 1892 – John Rannells Surveys Idlewild in Preparation for His Toll Road
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post December 15th, 1892 – John Rannells Surveys Idlewild in Preparation for His Toll Road appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
John Rannells surveys Idlewild to determine a route for his future toll road. He proposes building the road “…above Marvin’s cabin, following the south side of the canon…”

A temperary road, however, could be improvised on a good grade quite cheaply. Commencing at the mouth of Dixon’s gulch, we followed the old Handy survey for four miles, to the crossing of the Big Thompson near the mouth of North Fork , setting stakes every two hundred feet, and taking notes of the work in every section. We found the route entirely practical and no very difficult work. This survey leaves the creek twice and passes over two hill, the first being about 2000 feet in length with a grade of ten feet to the hundred all the way, coming down on the other side by a gentle grade of about seven feet to the hundred into the Walker flats. The next hill is longer but not so steep, coming down with a gentle grade into the canon above Marvin’s cabin, following the south side of the canon to near the upper end  where a favorable place for crossing at an island with a span of about 16 feet to the island and about 50 feet on the other side, would land us for the first time on the north side of the Big Thompson – whence a wagon trail seemed to extend on up to the North Fork, less than a mile distant.

Loveland Reporter – December 15th, 1892

The post December 15th, 1892 – John Rannells Surveys Idlewild in Preparation for His Toll Road appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.

item.title: Manhunt for Frank Marvin at Idlewild
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post Manhunt for Frank Marvin at Idlewild appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
A group of men under the direction of the Fort Collins Sheriff were sent to present day Idlewild following John Madison’s murder. They found traps, furs and personal effects at his rock shelter. Frank Marvin had abandoned his belongings and would never return to Idlewild.

Jim Sweeney, the first sheriff of Larimer County, instructed his deputy to get several men and arrest Marvin. Among these men were John Lowe, George Galucia, Pat O’Hara and J. J. Thornton. Thornton and O’Hara went up into Rattlesnake park and crossed over into what is now Waltonia. Galucia, Lowe and the deputy sheriff went over Ute Pass and up the river. Upon a small hill where Lee Bonnell’s house now stands, they stopped and held council. (Since both Lee and his wife are dead, the house on the hill has passed to other hands.) John Lowe was a southern soldier, and the deputy told him to go down and get Marvin, while he and Galucia watched that Marvin did not get away. (Just like a deputy). “No,” said Lowe to the Deputy. ”You go and get him and let Galucia and I do the watching.” At that time O’Hara and Thornton were on the hill directly south of the Light or Municipal Dam. They saw the other three move away, and they too, took a back track.

Frend Neville, Marvin Flats,
Over Hill and Vale; In The Evening Shadows of Colorado’s Longs Peak,
1956

The post Manhunt for Frank Marvin at Idlewild appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.

item.title: Frank Marvin Steals a Mule and Brings it to Idlewild
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post Frank Marvin Steals a Mule and Brings it to Idlewild appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
Sometime in 1878 Frank Marvin found a mule in the Buckhorn canyon and brought it back to his Idlewild home.

“It seems that Marvin had taken up a mule belonging to one of Matsons [Madisons] neighbors and kept it picketed up in the foot-hills claiming it as his own. Matson [Madison] went and got the mule and took it home.”

Fort Collins Courier – November 9th, 1878

The post Frank Marvin Steals a Mule and Brings it to Idlewild appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.

item.title: October, 1878 – Don Anderson and John Madison Confront Frank Marvin at Idlewild
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Dauth
item.description: The post October, 1878 – Don Anderson and John Madison Confront Frank Marvin at Idlewild appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
item.content:encoded:
John Madison and Don Anderson go to Frank Marvin’s Idlewild home and confront him regarding the stolen mule. They rough up Frank, confiscate his guns and retrieve the mule.

One day Anderson and Madison went to Marvin’s rock home, and a fight followed. While Anderson was treating Marvin rough, Madison went in and took Marvin’s guns.

Frend Neville, Marvin Flats,
Over Hill and Vale; In The Evening Shadows of Colorado’s Longs Peak,
1956

The post October, 1878 – Don Anderson and John Madison Confront Frank Marvin at Idlewild appeared first on Idlewild Lodge.
